heroText: Port-au-Prince is a capital city in Haiti, combining local landmarks, event context, transport access and seasonal reasons to plan a visit.

highlights.name: Port-au-Prince
highlights.text: Port-au-Prince is a capital city in Haiti, combining local landmarks, event context, transport access and seasonal reasons to plan a visit.

history.text: Thus, in 1749, a new city was built, Port-au-Prince The Place du Champ-de-Mars—the site of a number of historically notable structures in the centre of the city—was hit hard by the 2010 earthquake.

French colonial commissioner Étienne Polverel named the city Port-Républicain on 23 September 1793 "in order that the inhabitants be kept continually in mind of the obligations which the French Revolution imposed on them".
In 1804, it became the capital of newly independent Haïti.
When Jean-Jacques Dessalines was assassinated in 1806, Port-au-Prince became the capital of the mulatto-dominated south (Cap-Haïtien was the capital of the black-dominated north).
It was re-established as the capital of all of Haiti when the country was unified again in 1820 Port-au-Prince was the centre of the political and intellectual life of the country and is the.

Other notable landmarks include the Cathedral of Notre Dame and the adjacent colonial cathedral, both of which also collapsed in the 2010 earthquake, and the National Archives, National Library, and National Museum. Foundation of.

history.label: Regional role
history.text: It was later renamed back to Port-au-Prince by Jacques I, Emperor of Haiti.

history.label: Regional role
history.text: When Haiti was divided between a kingdom in the north and a republic in the south, Port-au-Prince was the capital of the republic, under the leadership of Alexandre Pétion.

history.label: Regional role
history.text: Henri Christophe renamed the city Port-aux-Crimes after the assassination of Jacques I at Pont Larnage (now known as Pont-Rouge, and located north of the city). Taino Period The Port-au-Prince area was part of the.

history.label: Regional role
history.text: Direct Spanish rule over the area having been established, Ovando founded a settlement not far from the coast (west of Etang Saumâtre), ironically named Santa Maria de la Paz Verdadera, which would be abandoned.

history.label: Regional role
history.text: While the first French presence in Hôpital, the region later to contain Port-au-Prince was that of the flibustiers; as the region became a real French colony, the colonial administration began to worry about the.

history.label: Regional role
history.text: By then, the colonial administration was convinced that a capital needed to be chosen, in order to better control the French portion of Hispaniola (Saint-Domingue).

history.label: Regional role
history.text: Most of the central historic area of the city was destroyed, including Haiti's prized Cathédrale de Port-au-Prince, the capital building, Legislative Palace (the parliament building), Palace of Justice (Supreme Court building), several ministerial buildings.

history.label: Regional role
history.text: Aid was delivered to Port-au-Prince by numerous nations and voluntary groups as part of a global relief effort.
